perturbation.original_text: Subcontractor shall be permitted to subcontract any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.changed_text: Subcontractor shall be permitted to delegate any part of the Work without the prior written notification and consent of EDGE. However, Subcontractor will follow subcontracting requirements and processes of EDGE and provide joint access to subcontractor database and information concerning quality control. All Work performed by a Lower-tier Subcontractor shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor. Subcontractor agrees not to hire any Lower-tier Subcontractor to whose employment EDGE reasonably objects.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'subcontract' is changed to 'delegate'. While seemingly similar, delegation might imply a transfer of responsibility without a formal contractual agreement. This introduces ambiguity, especially considering later clauses. In the original text subcontracting part of the work without notification is acceptable, but the agreement requires the Subcontractor to still follow requirements from EDGE when subcontracting. Introducing the word delegation, will create uncertainty in enforcement, because by subcontracting, there is a clear flow of contract obligations where as if the Subcontractor delegates, it can be interpretated that there are no contract obligations tied to the delegated worker.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subcontractor shall not assign any right or interest under this Agreement (excepting monies due, or to become due) or delegate or subcontract any Work or other obligation to be performed or owed under this Agreement without prior consent of EDGE. Notwithstanding, Subcontractor may delegate or assign Work under this Agreement to a subsidiary operating entity. All Work performed by Subcontractor's Lower- tier Subcontractors shall be deemed Work performed by Subcontractor.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'subcontract' with 'delegate' in the clause outlining permissible actions. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text explicitly states 'Subcontractor shall not assign any right or interest... or delegate or subcontract any Work... without prior consent of EDGE.' By initially allowing delegation without notification (in the changed text), it directly contradicts the clause requiring prior consent for delegation elsewhere in the agreement. The original text allowed subcontracting with certain conditions; the changed text introduces 'delegation' which has different legal implications and creates a contradiction with the clause requiring prior consent for both subcontracting and delegation.

perturbation.original_text: All Work shall be performed on the basis of certain agreed upon prices specified in the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor. B. Payment. EDGE shall pay Subcontractor for Work completed in accordance with the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor. C. This Agreement shall not be construed as a "Pay When Paid" contract. EDGE's obligation to pay Subcontractor under this Agreement shall not be predicated upon the Customer approving or paying EDGE for the Work.
perturbation.changed_text: All Deliverables shall be performed on the basis of certain agreed upon prices specified in the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor. B. Payment. EDGE shall pay Subcontractor for Deliverables completed in accordance with the related Exhibit or individual Purchase Order once accepted by Subcontractor. C. This Agreement shall not be construed as a "Pay When Paid" contract. EDGE's obligation to pay Subcontractor under this Agreement shall not be predicated upon the Customer approving or paying EDGE for the Work.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Work' is replaced with 'Deliverables'. 'Work' is a broadly defined term referring to the scope of work agreed by the parties, whereas 'Deliverables' refers to the specific tangible or intangible output that is expected upon the end of the work process, and this change can lead to confusion about what the subcontractor will be paid for. With the perturbation of the term being swapped, it creates an uncertainty in enforcement due to the ambiguity in differentiating the Work scope agreed vs the expected deliverables at the end of the Work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subcontractor shall perform work for EDGE referred to as "Route Feasibility & Site Acquisition," "Materials Management," "OSP Construction," and "Other Work" as outlined in the attached Exhibits A, B, C and D and as described and set forth in Purchase Orders.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Work', a broad term encompassing all activities performed by the subcontractor. The changed text replaces 'Work' with 'Deliverables', a narrower term referring to specific outputs. The contradicted text then specifies the 'work' that the subcontractor will perform. By limiting payment to 'Deliverables', the changed text introduces a contradiction because the subcontractor is obligated to perform 'Work' (Route Feasibility & Site Acquisition, Materials Management, OSP Construction, and Other Work) but may only be paid for tangible or intangible 'Deliverables' resulting from that work. This creates an ambiguity and potential for non-payment for activities that constitute 'Work' but are not considered 'Deliverables', thus it creates a direct conflict and falls under Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Subcontractor shall obtain at its own cost and expense and maintain the insurance in full force and effect during the term of the Agreement as required herein. A copy of the (i) certificate(s) of insurance and (ii) endorsements, acceptable to EDGE, shall be submitted to EDGE prior to commencement of any Work and renewals or replacements of such certificates shall be so delivered at least 30 days prior to the expiration or termination of each such policy. A copy of the insurance policies shall promptly be made available to EDGE upon EDGE' request. Subcontractor expressly acknowledges while EDGE retains the right to review the insurance provided by Subcontractor and' Lower-tier Subcontractors, EDGE is not obligated to perform such review. In addition, EDGE exercise of such right is for EDGE' benefit alone and shall confer no rights to Subcontractor or to any third party. EDGE's acceptance of or failure to object to the submitted documents does not constitute approval of coverage that is not in compliance with this Agreement or as acceptance or affirmation of the adequacy or applicability of such insurance.
perturbation.changed_text: Subcontractor shall obtain at its own cost and expense and maintain the bonds in full force and effect during the term of the Agreement as required herein. A copy of the (i) certificate(s) of insurance and (ii) endorsements, acceptable to EDGE, shall be submitted to EDGE prior to commencement of any Work and renewals or replacements of such certificates shall be so delivered at least 30 days prior to the expiration or termination of each such policy. A copy of the insurance policies shall promptly be made available to EDGE upon EDGE' request. Subcontractor expressly acknowledges while EDGE retains the right to review the insurance provided by Subcontractor and' Lower-tier Subcontractors, EDGE is not obligated to perform such review. In addition, EDGE exercise of such right is for EDGE' benefit alone and shall confer no rights to Subcontractor or to any third party. EDGE's acceptance of or failure to object to the submitted documents does not constitute approval of coverage that is not in compliance with this Agreement or as acceptance or affirmation of the adequacy or applicability of such insurance.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'insurance' is replaced with 'bonds'. Insurance provides financial protection against specific risks and 'bonds' refers to financial guarantees ensuring obligations are fulfilled. Switching these terms creates uncertainty as the specific coverage needed is undefined. Due to the perturbation being made, it now becomes more ambiguous as to the obligation, where there is financial coverage against risk vs having financial guarentee that obligation is being fulfilled, as insurance would cover the losses arising from risks, but a bond provides assurance that an obligation is being fulfilled, creating uncertainty in enforcement due to the differences in financial implications for the parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) Commercial General Liability Insurance providing the limits of coverage written on an occurrence basis, in no event less than $1,000,000 combined single limit and $2,000,000 in the aggregate for personal and bodily injury and death arising therefrom and Broad Form property damage arising out of any one occurrence in connection with the Work or any part thereof, which insurance shall include coverage with the same minimum limits for contractual liability and completed operations liability. General Liability and Umbrella/Excess Liability policies must have "per location or per project" aggregates.
perturbation.justification: The original text required the subcontractor to maintain insurance. The changed text requires the subcontractor to maintain bonds. The contradicted text specifies the requirements for 'Commercial General Liability Insurance', which is a type of insurance. Because the requirement was changed from 'insurance' to 'bonds', the subcontractor is no longer explicitly required to maintain the insurance policy detailed in the contradicted text, creating a contradiction. The perturbed text creates an ambiguity around whether insurance is required at all, directly conflicting with the contradicted text that specifies the type and amount of insurance required, thus leading to an In-Text Contradiction due to Misaligned Terminology.
